checks.finding: The policy matrix fixes input sources, normalization, ordered triggers, plural forms, exclusions, diagnostics, and exit statuses; generation and human-copy contracts fix the remaining implementer choices.
The full-track escalation, runtime-native module requirement, no-runtime-JSON constraint, mutable batch manifest, and immutable R-10 runner are mutually consistent.

Acceptance coverage includes false denials, real writes, first/middle/last signature mutations, malformed generation, poisoned module loading, unavailable policy input, and human-copy escape/tamper cases.
Open Questions is None and the PowerShell, full-track, generated-module, and protected-copy decisions are recorded as user decisions with deterministic constraints.
R-10 targets, human-only copy, manifest validation before copy, and no sudo bypass are explicit and have named acceptance verification.
The implementation-policy reviewer can determine all required modules, inputs, failure modes, CI ordering, and constraints without inventing product behavior.
